items.title: Arrive KLIA
items.detail: Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KUL). KLIA Ekspres train to KL Sentral takes 28 minutes — RM55 one-way.
items.directions: KLIA Ekspres departs from KLIA Arrival Hall, Level 1. Buy tickets at the station or in advance online.

items.title: Check in — KLCC / Bukit Bintang area
items.detail: Stay in the Golden Triangle area for walkability to Petronas Towers, Jalan Alor, and KLCC Park.
items.directions: Most hotels in KLCC area are walking distance from KL Sentral via MRT or Monorail.

items.title: Dinner: Jalan Alor Night Market
items.detail: KL's most famous hawker street. Char kway teow, grilled seafood, satay — eat your way down the strip. Budget RM30-60/person.
items.directions: Jalan Alor is off Jalan Bukit Bintang, a 10-minute walk from KLCC area hotels.

items.title: Breakfast: Nasi Lemak at Village Park
items.detail: Village Park Restaurant in Damansara is the benchmark nasi lemak in KL. Worth the trip. RM10-15/person.
items.directions: Grab/Taxi from KLCC area, about 15-20 minutes.

items.title: Batu Caves
items.detail: 272 steps to the main cave temple. Free entry to the cave itself. Sri Subramaniar Swamy Temple at the base. The golden Lord Murugan statue is the tallest in Malaysia.
items.directions: KTM Komuter train from KL Sentral to Batu Caves station, 30 minutes.

items.title: Petronas Towers Observation Deck
items.detail: Tickets RM85/person. Skybridge on Level 41 and observation deck on Level 86. Book online in advance — sells out fast.
items.directions: KLCC MRT station is directly connected to the Petronas Twin Towers via underground walkway.

items.title: Dinner: Atmosphere 360 or Hakka Restaurant
items.detail: Atmosphere 360 in KL Tower for a splurge (RM200+/person) or Hakka Restaurant in Jalan Kia Peng for excellent Chinese-Malaysian cuisine (RM60-100/person).
items.directions: Both a short Grab ride from KLCC.

items.title: Merdeka Square & Sultan Abdul Samad Building
items.detail: The historic heart of KL where Malaysian independence was declared in 1957. The Sultan Abdul Samad Building with its copper domes is the photo-postcard of colonial KL.
items.directions: Masjid Jamek MRT or LRT station, 5-minute walk.

items.title: Petaling Street (Chinatown)
items.detail: KL's Chinatown market for souvenirs, street food, and atmosphere. Haggling expected. Best for char siu pau, chee cheong fun, and fresh fruit.
items.directions: Pasar Seni MRT station, directly at Chinatown.

items.title: National Museum (Muzium Negara)
items.detail: Best overview of Malaysian history and culture. Entrance RM5 for foreigners. Allow 2 hours.
items.directions: Kuala Lumpur railway station area, walking distance from Pasar Seni.

items.title: KTM ETS Train: KL Sentral → Butterworth
items.detail: ETS Gold service takes approximately 3.5 hours. Book in advance at KTM Berhad website. RM60-80 one-way.
items.directions: KL Sentral station — ETS trains depart from the main platforms. Arrive 30 minutes early.

items.title: Butterworth Ferry to Georgetown
items.detail: Penang Ferry Service runs every 20-30 minutes. RM1.20 one-way (foot passenger). 10-minute crossing.
items.directions: Butterworth Ferry Terminal is adjacent to the train station. Follow signs.

items.title: Check in — George Town Heritage Zone
items.detail: Stay in or near the UNESCO heritage zone for walking access to street art, hawker centers, and colonial architecture.
items.directions: George Town UNESCO zone is compact — most heritage hotels are walkable from the ferry terminal.

items.title: Dinner: Gurney Drive Hawker Centre
items.detail: Penang's most famous hawker stretch. Penang char kway teow, assam laksa, rojak, cendol. Budget RM25-40/person.
items.directions: Grab ride from George Town heritage area, about 15 minutes.

items.title: Breakfast: Penang Kopitiam
items.detail: Traditional Penang-style kopitiam coffee shop. Kaya toast, half-boiled eggs, and the best local coffee in Malaysia. Try Sin Yin Lee or Toh Soon Cafe.
items.directions: George Town heritage zone, walking distance from most hotels.

items.title: Ernest Zacharevic Street Art Walk
items.detail: George Town's iconic iron rod and mural street art trail. Pick up a free art map from tourist info. The 'Children on Bicycle' mural is the most photographed in Malaysia.
items.directions: Start at the Clan Jetties and work through the UNESCO zone. Wear walking shoes.

items.title: Lunch: Penang Assam Laksa at Air Itam
items.detail: Michelin Bib Gourmand-recognized. The original assam laksa at Ayer Itam market is considered the definitive version. RM6-8/bowl.
items.directions: Grab ride to Ayer Itam market, about 20 minutes from George Town.

items.title: Khoo Kongsi Clan House
items.detail: The most ornate Hokkien clan house in Malaysia. Intricate carvings, porcelain decorations, and a history going back to 1851. Entrance RM10.
items.directions: Cannon Square, George Town heritage zone, walking distance from most hotels.

items.title: Flight: Penang (PEN) → Langkawi (LGK)
items.detail: AirAsia or FireFly. Flight time 35-45 minutes. Book in advance — prices rise fast for New Year's period. Alternatively, ferry from Penang takes 2.5 hours (RM80-100).
items.directions: Penang International Airport is 15km south of George Town. Grab or taxi, about 30 minutes.

items.title: Check in — Pantai Cenang Beach Resort
items.detail: Langkawi's main beach strip. Mid-range resorts from RM300-600/night. Budget guesthouses from RM80-150/night. Duty-free island so alcohol is cheap.
items.directions: Langkawi airport is centrally located — most beach resorts 20-30 minutes by taxi.

items.title: Langkawi Cable Car & Sky Bridge
items.detail: Cable car to the summit of Gunung Mat Cincang (708m). The Sky Bridge is a curved pedestrian bridge over a 100m drop. Cable car + Sky Bridge RM55/person.
items.directions: Oriental Village, Burau Bay — Grab or rental car, about 20 minutes from Pantai Cenang.

items.title: New Year's Eve at Pantai Cenang
items.detail: Langkawi beach parties run all night. Multiple beach bars and clubs along Pantai Cenang organize countdown parties. Entry fees vary RM50-200 depending on venue.
items.directions: Pantai Cenang beach strip — walk between venues.

items.title: Island Hopping Tour
items.detail: Classic Langkawi island hopping: Pulau Dayang Bunting (freshwater lake), Pulau Beras Basah (white sand), eagle feeding at Singa Besar. Half day tour RM35-50/person.
items.directions: Most resorts and hotels can arrange island hopping tours. Or book at Telaga Harbour or Kuah jetty.

items.title: Duty-Free Shopping at Kuah Town
items.detail: Langkawi is a duty-free island — chocolate, cigarettes, liquor, and electronics are significantly cheaper. Kuah town has the main duty-free outlets.
items.directions: Kuah is the main town, about 25-30 minutes from Pantai Cenang by taxi.

items.title: Sunset Dinner: Scarborough Fish & Chips or Yellow Cafe
items.detail: Scarborough Fish & Chips is an institution on Pantai Cenang — fresh catch, classic preparation. Yellow Cafe for excellent seafood pasta and sunset views. RM40-80/person.
items.directions: Both on Pantai Cenang beach strip.

items.title: Final morning — Pantai Tengah or Tanjung Rhu
items.detail: Tanjung Rhu in the north of Langkawi is the quietest and most beautiful beach on the island. Worth the 40-minute drive for a final morning swim.
items.directions: Rental car or taxi to Tanjung Rhu, north coast of Langkawi.

items.title: Depart Langkawi (LGK)
items.detail: Langkawi to KL (KUL) direct flights run multiple times daily. Alternatively, fly to Penang and connect.
items.directions: Langkawi International Airport, central location. Allow 90 minutes before departure.
